query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "many-shot cot-icl: making in-context learning truly learn" linguistics
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (f) as it investigates the core mechanism of "Chain-of-Thought" (CoT) reasoning and its scaling properties (long vs. short), which are the fundamental variables in the user's question about "Many-Shot CoT-ICL." Although the paper is a survey rather than a specific follow-up experiment, it provides the canonical definitions, taxonomies, and empirical phenomena (like inference-time scaling and overthinking) that any literature review on extending CoT-ICL must
